2025.title: Chairwoman
2025.name: Sama El-Badrawy
2025.description: The Chairman oversees the entire TEDxGUC operation, ensuring the event aligns with TED's mission of spreading ideas worth sharing.
2025.committeeDescription: The Chairman's office sets the strategic direction for the entire TEDxGUC organization. Responsible for external partnerships, TED licensing compliance, and ensuring every division works in harmony toward delivering a world-class event.

children.title: Curator — Talks
children.name: Yehia Amr
children.description: Leads the Talks division, curating speakers and ensuring content quality aligns with the event's theme.
children.committeeDescription: The Talks division is the heart of TEDxGUC. This team scouts, selects, and develops speakers — ensuring every talk is compelling, well-researched, and delivered with impact.

children.title: Coaching
children.name: Mariam Adel
children.description: Coaches speakers through rehearsals and presentation skills workshops.
children.committeeDescription: The Coaching committee works one-on-one with speakers to refine their narratives, improve delivery, and ensure every talk resonates with the audience.
children.memberBio: Mariam is a psychology student with experience in public speaking coaching and debate.

children.title: Research & Production
children.name: Hassan Tarek
children.description: Conducts research to support speakers and manages the production pipeline.
children.committeeDescription: Research & Production ensures every talk is backed by solid research and produced to the highest standards — from script development to stage design.
children.memberBio: Hassan is a political science student with a keen eye for detail and a love for investigative research.

children.title: Curator — Operations
children.name: Kareem Shawish
children.description: Manages all operational logistics, event planning, and day-of execution.
children.committeeDescription: Operations is the backbone of TEDxGUC. From venue logistics to vendor management, this division ensures every detail is accounted for so the event runs flawlessly.

children.title: Events Management
children.name: Dina Sherif
children.description: Plans and executes all event activities.
children.committeeDescription: Events Management handles venue coordination, scheduling, and on-site attendee experience from doors-open to closing remarks.
children.memberBio: Dina is an architecture student who brings her spatial awareness and creativity to event design.

children.title: Partnerships
children.name: Ali Mahmoud
children.description: Builds relationships with sponsors and stakeholders.
children.committeeDescription: Partnerships secures sponsorships and strategic alliances that fund and elevate the TEDxGUC experience.
children.memberBio: Ali is a finance student with strong networking skills and a talent for negotiation.

children.title: Logistics
children.name: Rana Hossam
children.description: Handles transportation, equipment, catering, and venue setup.
children.committeeDescription: Logistics manages the physical infrastructure of the event — from equipment rental and catering to transportation and setup.
children.memberBio: Rana is an industrial engineering student who thrives on solving complex logistical puzzles.

children.title: Curator — OD
children.name: Hazem Talhawy
children.description: Focuses on team growth, internal culture, and organizational excellence.
children.committeeDescription: Organizational Development nurtures the TEDxGUC team itself — running training programs, building culture, and ensuring every member grows alongside the organization.

children.title: Associate Development
children.name: Youssef Gamal
children.description: Develops training programs for new associates.
children.committeeDescription: Associate Development creates onboarding programs, workshops, and mentorship tracks for new team members.
children.memberBio: Youssef is an HR management student who believes in investing in people as the key to organizational success.

children.title: Associate Experience
children.name: Farida Nasser
children.description: Ensures a positive experience for all team members.
children.committeeDescription: Associate Experience designs team-bonding activities, feedback systems, and wellness initiatives to keep morale high.
children.memberBio: Farida is a psychology student dedicated to creating inclusive and supportive team environments.

children.title: Curator — Marketing
children.name: Jana Nader
children.description: Directs all marketing, branding, and communication strategies.
children.committeeDescription: Marketing & Coordination amplifies the TEDxGUC brand through social media, content creation, and integrated campaigns that drive engagement and ticket sales.

children.title: Media & Design
children.name: Nada Emad
children.description: Creates all visual content and social media assets.
children.committeeDescription: Media & Design produces the visual identity of TEDxGUC — from social posts and event branding to video content and stage graphics.
children.memberBio: Nada is a digital media student with a portfolio spanning photography, motion graphics, and UI design.

children.title: Campaigns & Copy
children.name: Ziad Ayman
children.description: Develops marketing campaigns and writes compelling copy.
children.committeeDescription: Campaigns & Copywriting crafts the voice of TEDxGUC — writing copy, planning content calendars, and executing multi-channel marketing campaigns.
children.memberBio: Ziad is a journalism student who combines data-driven strategy with creative storytelling.

events.recap: Our 2025 Spring event brought together 8 speakers across technology, health, design, and culture to explore how we can redefine the future. With over 1000 attendees, the event featured immersive experiences, networking sessions, and thought-provoking talks that challenged conventional thinking.
TEDxGUC 'Uncharted' explored the unknown territories of human potential. From design thinking to mental health advocacy, our speakers pushed audiences to venture beyond their comfort zones.

speakers.name: Karim Nabil
speakers.talkTitle: Breaking the Silence on Mental Health

events.recap: Our 'Mosaic' event celebrated the beauty of diverse perspectives coming together. Speakers shared stories of entrepreneurship, storytelling, and cultural preservation.

upcomingEvent.description: Ideas that ignite change. TEDxGUC 'Catalyst' will bring together innovators, artists, and thinkers who spark transformation in their communities.

upcomingSchedule.title: Doors Open & Registration
upcomingSchedule.description: Check-in, grab your badge, and enjoy coffee.

upcomingSchedule.title: Session 1 — Spark
upcomingSchedule.description: Three talks exploring the origins of transformative ideas.

upcomingSchedule.title: Networking Break
upcomingSchedule.description: Connect with fellow attendees over refreshments.

upcomingSchedule.title: Session 2 — Ignite
upcomingSchedule.description: Three talks on turning ideas into action.

upcomingSchedule.title: Lunch Break
upcomingSchedule.description: Catered lunch and interactive experience booths.

upcomingSchedule.title: Session 3 — Transform
upcomingSchedule.description: Three closing talks on lasting impact and legacy.

upcomingSchedule.title: Closing & Networking
upcomingSchedule.description: Closing remarks followed by open networking.

upcomingFAQs.question: What is the dress code?
upcomingFAQs.answer: Smart casual is recommended. Please dress comfortably as the event runs for a full day. Avoid overly casual attire like shorts or flip-flops.

upcomingFAQs.question: Are refunds available?
upcomingFAQs.answer: Tickets are non-refundable but transferable. If you can't attend, you may transfer your ticket to someone else by contacting us at least 48 hours before the event.

upcomingFAQs.question: What is the filming policy?
upcomingFAQs.answer: Professional filming will take place throughout the event. By attending, you consent to being photographed or filmed. Personal recording of talks is not permitted.

upcomingFAQs.question: Is parking available?
upcomingFAQs.answer: Free parking is available at the GUC campus. We recommend arriving early as spaces fill up quickly.

upcomingFAQs.question: Can I bring a guest?
upcomingFAQs.answer: Each ticket admits one person. If you'd like to bring a guest, they will need their own ticket.
